response.justification: The candidate is a versatile senior full-stack engineer with genuine systems experience (low-latency DLang microservices handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby, Kubernetes-deployable platforms), but he falls well short of the role's core minimum qualifications: roughly 7 months of professional C++ at National Instruments versus the required 8 years, and no evidence of formal technical leadership of a cloud compute platform or of mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers in a lead capacity. His career centers on web/product engineering in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang rather than large-scale compute infrastructure, storage, or hardware architecture, making him a poor fit for this specific technical-lead position despite strong general craftsmanship.
strengths: Real high-throughput distributed-systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, low-latency services)
Strong engineering-practice foundation: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing tools authorship, and teaching/workshop experience
Broad polyglot background with long-running open-source platform ownership (GISCollective) deployed on Kubernetes
concerns: Far below the required 8 years of C++ experience — only brief professional C++ exposure
No demonstrated technical-lead role owning strategy for large-scale cloud/compute infrastructure
Limited production cloud architecture depth (mostly application-level AWS/K8s usage rather than platform/compute engineering)

key_factors.factor: Lack of formal cloud-platform technical leadership
